description: Iraq invades Kuwait, triggering the Gulf War and removing significant OPEC supply from the market.
US-led coalition launches the air war against Iraq; oil prices crash as fears of a prolonged war ease.
Currency collapses across Southeast Asia sharply reduce regional oil demand and global growth expectations.
Terrorist attacks on the US create short-term demand shock and long-run geopolitical risk premium in oil markets.
US-led invasion of Iraq disrupts Iraqi oil production and raises Middle East supply risk.
Brent crude peaks near $147/barrel amid strong demand and speculation, just before the global financial crisis.
Lehman Brothers collapse triggers a global recession, causing oil demand and prices to crash sharply.
Political uprisings across the Middle East and North Africa, including civil war in Libya, disrupt regional oil supply.

event_name: OPEC Decision Not to Cut Production
description: OPEC maintains output despite oversupply from US shale growth, accelerating the 2014-2016 price collapse.
Implementation of the Iran nuclear deal lifts sanctions, allowing Iranian oil back onto global markets.
OPEC and non-OPEC producers (OPEC+) agree to coordinated production cuts to support falling prices.

event_name: US Withdrawal from Iran Nuclear Deal
description: US exits the JCPOA and reimposes sanctions on Iranian oil exports, tightening expected supply.
Drone/missile attacks on Abqaiq and Khurais temporarily knock out roughly half of Saudi oil production.
OPEC+ talks collapse; Saudi Arabia launches a price war, flooding the market with supply.

event_name: COVID-19 Demand Collapse (Negative WTI / Brent low)
description: Global lockdowns collapse oil demand; US WTI futures briefly turn negative and Brent falls to $9.12, its lowest in decades.
Russia's invasion of Ukraine triggers sanctions on Russian energy exports and major global supply uncertainty.
